# José Ramón Martínez Saavedra

> VP of Innovation and Lead Scientist at Quside Technologies. Physicist specializing in quantum random number generation, post-quantum cryptography, photonics, and cybersecurity.

## About

José Ramón Martínez Saavedra is a physicist and technologist based in Barcelona, Spain. He holds a PhD in Photonics (Cum Laude) from Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya (UPC) and ICFO — The Institute of Photonic Sciences (2018), and an MBA from Valar Institute (2025). His academic roots are in Physics at Universidad Complutense de Madrid (UCM).

Since 2018 he serves as VP of Innovation and Lead Scientist at Quside Technologies, where he leads R&D strategy in quantum random number generation (QRNG), post-quantum cryptography (PQC), and high-performance computing. He manages an international patent portfolio of 30+ families (co-inventor in 8) and coordinates competitive public funding including Horizon Europe and CDTI projects.

## Research Output

- 22 publications (20 peer-reviewed + 2 theses) in Nature, ACS Photonics, Physical Review B, Nano Letters, PNAS, npj Quantum Information, APL Quantum, Physical Review Applied, and others (2014–2026)
- 8 patent families as co-inventor across US, EP, CN, JP jurisdictions (2018–2023)
- Research topics: quantum random number generation, post-quantum cryptography, graphene plasmonics, nanophotonics, quantum computing, spin glasses, Monte Carlo methods

## Teaching

- Lecturer, "Software Architecture for Quantum Computers" — UPC Postgraduate Course in Quantum Engineering (2021–present)
- Invited seminars at Universidad Complutense de Madrid, Università di Bari, OCP Global Summit, QSecDef, Akademia (Bankinter)
- Mentoring: UPC PAE (ETSETB), ESADE Tech2X
- PhD thesis supervisor at UPC/ICFO (1 completed, 1 in progress)
- Master's thesis supervisor (4 completed)

- Quside Technologies S.L. — VP of Innovation, Lead Scientist (2018–present)
- Universitat Politècnica de Catalunya (UPC) — Postgraduate lecturer (2021–present)
- ICFO — PhD thesis co-supervisor
- Universidad Complutense de Madrid — Licenciado en Física (2008–2013)
